content.claim: Finland's 1972–1977 comprehensive school reform delayed tracking from age 11 to age 16, eliminating early selection into vocational and general tracks.
content.context: Delaying academic selection beyond age 12 in Hungary and extending common schooling to age 16, bundled with university-based teacher-education reform and protected against school-choice-driven substitution of formal tracking with within-school segregation, would be expected to reduce achievement gaps and raise intergenerational mobility, but only if the full policy package is implemented coherently.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
